pattern: Exactly one append creates the extra slot, the first n positions cost their direct distances, and the appended value is cheapest when copied from a source partway along its own edit path.
hints: Since the result is one element longer, exactly one append happens and it lands at the end.
The first n targets line up positionally with the originals, costing the sum of absolute differences.
For the appended slot, you may copy any index at any value along its journey, so clamp the appended target into each source's [start,end] interval and take the cheapest, then add one for the append.

edge_inputs.description: appended target far from every source

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    n1 = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    n2 = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n + 1)]
    return [n1, n2]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minOperations(self, nums1, nums2):
        n = len(nums1)
        full = sum(abs(nums2[i] - nums1[i]) for i in range(n))
        target = nums2[n]
        best = None
        for i in range(n):
            lo = min(nums1[i], nums2[i])
            hi = max(nums1[i], nums2[i])
            # try every reachable base x; extra cost on i if x leaves the interval
            x = min(max(target, lo), hi)
            cost = abs(target - x)
            if best is None or cost < best:
                best = cost
        return full + best + 1
